channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Oregon city at heart of high court homelessness ruling blocked from enforcing camping ban
item.description: An Oregon judge has blocked the city at the heart of a U.S. Supreme Court ruling on homelessness from enforcing its camping ban unless it meets certain conditions.
item.dc:creator: Claire Rush, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk says xAI buys social media platform X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said on Friday his artificial intelligence firm xAI has bought his social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Voice of America wins in court, for now, as judge blocks Trump administration from firing staff
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Voice of America can’t be silenced just yet. A federal judge on Friday halted the Trump administration’s efforts to dismantle the eight-decade-old U.S. government-funded international news service, calling the move a “classic case of arbitrary and capricious decision making.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin attorney general sues Elon Musk to block $1 million payment offers
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin’s Democratic attorney general asked a court on Friday to block billionaire Elon Musk from handing out $1 million checks to voters this weekend, just days before the state’s hotly contested Supreme Court race was to be decided.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jury from outside of Memphis will hear the case of 3 former officers charged in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — A jury from outside of Memphis will be seated in the state court trial of three former police officers charged with second-degree murder in the beating death of Tyre Nichols after a traffic stop in 2023, a judge ruled Friday. Shelby County Judge James Jones Jr.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The top-rated seafood restaurant in every state, according to Yelp
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: David Childs, architect of One World Trade Center that rose on Twin Towers site after 9/11, dies
item.description: David Childs, the lead architect of the One World Trade Center skyscraper that rose from the site where the Twin Towers collapsed in New York City during the 9/11 attacks, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wall Street tumbles on worries about slower economy, inflation
item.description: Another wipeout is swamping Wall Street Friday on worries about a potentially toxic mix of worsening inflation and a U.S. economy slowing because of households afraid to spend due to the global trade war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man suspected of setting fire to Tesla vehicles in Las Vegas arrested, police say
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A man suspected of setting fire to Tesla vehicles in Las Vegas and painting the word “resist” on the service center's doors has been arrested, police announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Second South Carolina inmate chooses execution by firing squad
item.description: A second South Carolina death row inmate, Mikal Mahdi, 41, has chosen execution by firing squad on April 11 following his guilty murder plea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social media influencer promotes clean eating amid federal push to ban food dyes
item.description: As efforts to ban the use of synthetic dyes in food gain ground in several states across the United States, a social media influencer is using her platform to help people become more aware of the harmful ingredients in their food.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell warned Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ asks Supreme Court to intervene in deportation flights case
item.description: The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Friday to allow it to invoke the Alien Enemies Act to carry out swift deportations.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump holds 'productive call' with new Canadian prime minister
item.description: President Trump spoke with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Friday for the first time since Carney was elected earlier in the month amid tensions between the two nations.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Yosemite National Park employee dies from mysterious injuries in staff housing
item.description: Multiple federal agencies are investigating a female park employee's suspicious death after she was found suffering from severe injuries inside Yosemite National Park.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Archeologists unearth signs from 1700s of British control in Florida after centuries of Spanish rule
item.description: The city of St. Augustine — with its centuries-old buildings and massive stone fort — is an enduring reminder of Spain’s storied history in Florida, going back generations before the United States even existed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah bans fluoride in public drinking water, a first in the US
item.description: Utah has become the first state to ban fluoride in public drinking water, despite widespread opposition from dentists and national health organizations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Green Bay Packer charged for alleged dogfighting
item.description: The standout college football running back played 14 games for the Green and Gold between 1994 and 1995.
item.dc:creator: Nick Witwer, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ investigating California universities over affirmative action
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) announced Thursday it will be investigating multiple California universities to ensure they are in compliance with a Supreme Court ruling banning affirmative action in admissions.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump restricts federal research funding, a lifeblood for colleges
item.description: After decades of partnership with the U.S. government, colleges are facing new doubts about the future of their federal funding.
item.dc:creator: SHARON LURYE and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ivy League anti-Israel ringleader Mahmoud Khalil fights deportation in New Jersey court
item.description: The Trump administration has sought to deport Columbia University graduate Mahmoud Khalil for leading the pro-Palestinian protests and sit-ins at the Ivy League institution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian scientist at Harvard Medical School detained by ICE at Boston airport
item.description: A Russian scientist working at Harvard Medical School was detained by ICE at Boston Logan International Airport when attempting to bring back frog embryos at the request of a professor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stanford, Cal and UCLA investigated in Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: The Trump administration has opened investigations into the admissions policies at Stanford University and three campuses within the University of California system, including UC Berkeley, UCLA and UC Irvine, the Department of Justice said Thursday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter swept up in US crackdown
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who express their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: First rain and then fire chase people from their homes in North and South Carolina
item.description: When Nicole Taylor and her family moved to their new home in the South Carolina mountains six months ago, the gorgeous view of Table Rock Mountain was the clincher.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Poll of scientists says large majority weighing leaving US
item.description: The scientific journal Nature poll found that 75.3 percent of scientists are considering leaving the U.S. after the administration cut funding for research.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: As many as 6 hurricanes forecast to strike US this season
item.description: “It only takes one landfall to create a devastating season,” AccuWeather chief meteorologist Jonathan Porter said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about the deadly air crash between a passenger jet and a US Army helicopter
item.description: A string of crashes this year along with a near miss have brought attention to air travel, which remains overwhelmingly safe.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rubio says at least 300 foreign students' visas have been revoked
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Thursday at least 300 foreign students have had their visas revoked amid the Trump administration's immigration agenda, a far higher number than was previously known.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione may get a laptop in jail to work on his defense in CEO murder case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge cleared the way Thursday for Luigi Mangione to get a laptop behind bars — if his jailers agree — so he can examine documents and other material in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sundance Film Festival is leaving Utah for Colorado, starting in 2027
item.description: Boulder has officially been unveiled as the city to host the Sundance Film Festival starting in 2027, according to a press release from the Sundance Institute.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, Fox 5

item.title: Toddler sneaks onto White House grounds
item.description: A toddler slipped through the White House fence on Wednesday, the Secret Service said in a post on the social platform X. “Just after 6:30 p.m. on Wednesday, U.S. Secret Service Uniformed Division officers observed a child slip through the White House north fence.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: Congress questions the FAA, US Army and NTSB over deadly midair collision in DC
item.description: The head of the National Transportation Safety Board appeared alongside officials from the Federal Aviation Administration and the Army's aviation division at a hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5

item.title: Bestselling author of 'The Vampire Diaries' dies at 66
item.description: Bestselling author L.J. Smith, best known for the book series "The Vampire Diaries," which also became a TV hit, has died at 66.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Stone, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How a 1970s Chicano art group defied the mainstream and made history
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When filmmaker Travis Gutiérrez Senger reflects on ASCO’s legacy, he quickly notes they were more than an art group; they created a movement, one with remarkable influence on Chicano art history. “That movement continues today, and it’s very expansive,” he says.
item.dc:creator: LESLIE AMBRIZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge won't lift block on Trump use of Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg declined to lift a restraining order barring the Trump administration from using the Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans, determining migrants are likely to succeed in pushing for hearings on whether they are in a gang.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9
